description: Declarative artifacts module consumed by the ESR runtime/CLI. Three sub-directories: (1) patterns/*.py — Python EDSL topology definitions (esr.command/node decorators) that compile to YAML via `esr cmd compile`; each pattern is an @command-decorated function whose body builds a DAG of node(...) calls chained via >> to express depends_on edges. (2) roles/<name>/{CLAUDE.md,settings.json} — Claude-Code role packs attached to a tmux cc_session at spawn; `esr workspace add --role <name>` copies the CLAUDE.md + settings.json into the session's workspace so a new cc_proxy/cc_session picks them up on init. (3) scenarios/*.yaml — end-to-end scenario manifests executed by `esr scenario run`, with setup/steps/teardown arrays whose each entry is a shell command + expect_exit + expect_stdout_match regex; driven by final_gate.sh --mock as gate #4. No module-local tests — the patterns are covered indirectly by py/tests/test_pattern_*.py in py-sdk-core.

key_symbols: tmux = node(id='tmux:{{thread_id}}', actor_type='tmux_proxy', adapter='cc_tmux', handler='tmux_proxy.on_msg', depends_on=[thread.id], init_directive={action: new_session}) :32
role-instructions: developer-assistance session using `reply` MCP tool, workspace=~/Workspace/esr
role-instructions: diagnostic session for final_gate.sh --live v2
type: scenario, name: e2e-esr-channel, mode: mock

setup: 8 steps (esrd start → wait :4001 → mock_feishu on :8101 → esr actors list smoke → compile feishu-app-session + feishu-thread-session → spawn_scenario_workers.sh root/alt/beta → workspace add esr-dev --role diagnostic → adapter add feishu-mock)
steps: [A-new-session, B-inbound-notify, C-reply-tool, D-at-addressing, E-session-killed, F-drain, G-deadletter-empty] (7 steps)
teardown: kill_scenario_workers.sh; mock_feishu pid kill; esrd stop; workspace remove esr-dev
setup: 6 steps (esrd start → wait :4001 → mock_feishu :8101 → esr actors list smoke → compile feishu-thread-session → spawn_scenario_workers.sh alpha/beta/gamma)
steps: [A-spawn-thread-alpha, B-spawn-thread-beta, C-actors-list-pids, D-stop-thread-alpha, E-telemetry-trace, F-drain-remaining, G-debug-pause-resume, H-deadletter-empty] (8 steps)
teardown: kill_scenario_workers.sh; mock_feishu kill; esrd stop

feishu-app-session.semantics: Singleton command binding one feishu_app_proxy actor to a single Feishu app instance. Instantiated once per (app_id, instance_name) pair.
feishu-app-session.runtime_achievement: PeerServer binds to the feishu adapter_runner; routes inbound Lark messages into per-thread actors, handles /new-thread and /new-session dispatch, fires InvokeCommand(feishu-thread-session) on /new-session, and implements @-addressing (lookup_active_thread_by_chat, @tag routing).

feishu-thread-session.semantics: Per-thread topology spawned on-demand by feishu_app's /new-thread or /new-session. Three actors in a linear depends_on chain.
feishu-thread-session.runtime_achievement: Topology.Instantiator activates thread-actor first, then runs tmux init_directive {action:new_session,start_cmd:scripts/esr-cc.sh,env:{ESR_WORKSPACE,ESR_SESSION_ID}} so the tmux session + cc binary exist before cc_session PeerServer binds. Rollback is clean: if tmux init fails, cc node never spawns (PRD 01 F13b). The @<tag> variable is user-facing addressing metadata scoped to the thread proxy only.

dev.claude_md: Developer-assistance session. Uses `reply` MCP tool to respond to Feishu messages. Workspace=~/Workspace/esr.
dev.activation: `esr workspace add <name> --role dev` copies roles/dev/{CLAUDE.md,settings.json} into the workspace; the cc_tmux init_directive spawns cc with ESR_WORKSPACE pointing at that workspace so Claude Code picks up the role instructions + MCP allow-list at session start.

diagnostic.claude_md: Diagnostic session used by final_gate.sh --live v2. Three hard-coded rules: (1) msg `ECHO-PROBE: <nonce>` → call _echo(nonce=<that>) — must NOT call reply directly, must NOT interpret; (2) `@<tag> ECHO-PROBE: <nonce>` → same (strip @<tag> routing prefix); (3) any other message → brief reply confirming receipt. Purpose: ESR transit verification only.
diagnostic.activation: `esr workspace add <name> --role diagnostic` — used by scenarios/e2e-esr-channel.yaml setup step #7 and by final_gate.sh --live; the _echo MCP tool replies with the nonce so the gate script can verify bidirectional ESR channel transit.

setup_summary: #2 wait for Phoenix bind on :4001 (60s loop)
#8 rm adapters.yaml + esr adapter add feishu-mock (auto-instantiates feishu-app-session)

steps.goal: esr cmd run feishu-app-session + mock_feishu /push_inbound '/new-session esr-dev tag=root' → thread:root/tmux:root/cc:root spawn
plain 'hello-probe' msg → feishu_app lookup_active_thread_by_chat → thread:root → Emit(esr-channel,notify_session,...) → SessionRegistry.push; sig-B in trace
mock_mcp_ctl tool_invoke(reply,chat_id=oc_m1,text=ack-C) → feishu_thread handle_info → Emit(feishu,send_message) → /sent_messages contains ack-C
spawn tag=alt then `@alt test-msg-D` routes to thread:alt only, not thread:root
esr cmd stop thread_id=root → cc:root PeerServer.terminate broadcasts session_killed
esr drain cascades stop across remaining topologies
deadletter flush + clean spawn/stop beta → queue stays empty

e2e-esr-channel.success_verdict: Every step returns exit 0 and stdout matches its expect_stdout_match regex verbatim (each embeds sig-B so LG-1 can't be defanged). Teardown is best-effort and never aborts the scenario.

steps.goal: esr cmd run feishu-thread-session thread_id=alpha chat_id=oc_mock_alpha
esr actors list shows BEAM pid <0.N.M> (sig-A)
spawn gamma → debug pause → actors inspect (paused=true) → debug resume

e2e-feishu-cc.success_verdict: Every step returns exit 0 and stdout matches its expect_stdout_match regex (each embeds one of sig-A..sig-F verbatim). Teardown best-effort.

compile_target.description: Patterns compile to YAML consumed by `esr cmd run <name>`. Compiled artifact path: ~/.esrd/default/commands/.compiled/<name>.yaml. Template vars like {{app_id}} resolve from --param k=v flags at submit time.
compile_target.compile_entry: esr cmd compile <name> -o <out.yaml> (py/src/esr/cli/main.py:646 cmd_compile) which calls esr.command.compile_to_yaml

interfaces.role_activation_path: `esr workspace add <name> --role R` (py/src/esr/cli/main.py:1216 workspace_add) writes ~/.esrd/default/workspaces.yaml with a role pointer; cc_tmux init_directive's env.ESR_WORKSPACE=<name> hands it to scripts/esr-cc.sh which lays out the cc workspace (CLAUDE.md + .claude/settings.json copied from roles/<R>/) before exec'ing claude-code.

internal_esr_modules_used_by_patterns: esr.command (@command decorator) — from `esr import command, node`
esr.node (node() constructor + >> operator for depends_on edges)
adapter_handlers_referenced_by_patterns: feishu_thread.on_msg (handler — called by feishu-thread-session thread node)
scenario_runtime_requirements: scripts/mock_mcp_ctl.py (MCP test-injection control for e2e-esr-channel C-reply-tool)
consumer_modules: py/src/esr/cli/main.py:1216 workspace_add --role — binds roles/<name>/ to a workspace

test_results.command: no own tests; covered indirectly by py-sdk-core test_pattern_*.py

steps: Write patterns/my-pattern.py with @command decorator + node(...) chain
esr cmd install patterns/my-pattern.py  (validates deps, writes compiled YAML)
esr cmd run my-pattern --param k=v  (runtime activates topology)

user_flows.flow: Attach a role to a cc workspace
steps: esr workspace add myspace --cwd /tmp/myspace --start-cmd scripts/esr-cc.sh --role <name> --chat oc_xxx:cli_mock:dm
When feishu_app handles /new-session myspace tag=X, the spawned cc_session picks up the role's CLAUDE.md + settings.json via ESR_WORKSPACE → scripts/esr-cc.sh
final_gate.sh --mock  OR  esr scenario run e2e-esr-channel
Runner executes each setup/step as `subprocess.run(shell=True)`; expect_exit + expect_stdout_match (regex) validated per entry
Teardown always runs; overall exit 0 iff every step's verdict is PASS
final_gate.sh --live sends `ECHO-PROBE: <nonce>` via Feishu; diagnostic CLAUDE.md instructs the cc session to call _echo(nonce) MCP tool; echo reply confirms full bidirectional ESR channel transit

notes: Patterns use a tiny Python EDSL (from `esr import command, node`): @command('name') registers the function in esr.command.COMMAND_REGISTRY, node(id=..., actor_type=..., adapter=..., handler=..., params=..., depends_on=..., init_directive=...) constructs topology nodes, and the `>>` operator (returning the RHS) chains depends_on relationships. Template vars like {{app_id}} and {{thread_id}} stay as literals in the compiled YAML and are resolved at submit time from --param k=v flags.

feishu-app-session is singleton (one per app_id). feishu-thread-session is parameterized per thread_id with chat_id/tag/workspace params. The tmux init_directive's env.ESR_WORKSPACE=='{{workspace}}' is the mechanism that hands the role's CLAUDE.md + settings.json to the newly-spawned cc session via scripts/esr-cc.sh.

The thread >> tmux >> cc chain guarantees correct startup order: thread actor starts first (it validates params + registers the chat binding), then tmux runs init_directive new_session (creates the tmux shell + launches scripts/esr-cc.sh), and only after tmux reports success does cc_proxy bind to the live cc_session process. Rollback on init_directive failure is handled by the Topology Instantiator (PRD 01 F13b).

Roles are deliberately minimal: a markdown file (system prompt / rules) + a JSON MCP permission allow-list. The diagnostic role's _echo tool is intentionally kept out of the dev allow-list — dev sessions must NOT be able to short-circuit human reasoning via _echo.

diagnostic CLAUDE.md rule #1 explicitly says 'Do NOT think, do not explain, do not call reply directly' — the _echo tool handles the reply itself with the synthetic nonce. This is critical for final_gate.sh --live's transit-latency probe to be reproducible.

Both scenarios embed a fixed sig-B regex 'actor_id=(thread|tmux|cc|feishu-app):[a-z0-9-]+' in every step that matters — this is the spec-v2.1 LG-1 'log-grep' signature that the gate script cannot tamper with unless it also tampers with runtime output. e2e-feishu-cc additionally tests sig-A (BEAM pid shape <0.N.M>) in its C step.

e2e-esr-channel is the v0.2 scenario (7 steps), exercising the new MCP channel stack (ChannelSocket + ChannelChannel + SessionRegistry + cc_mcp bridge + /new-session command + @-addressing + session_killed broadcast). e2e-feishu-cc is the v0.1 legacy scenario (8 steps), exercising the original feishu-thread-session topology without the MCP channel layer.

Both scenarios' setup sections kill stale mock_feishu instances before starting (e2e-esr-channel uses `pkill -f`, e2e-feishu-cc does not — relying on pidfile) — this re-run idempotency is important because final_gate.sh --mock may re-invoke on CI.

Module has zero module-local test files; coverage is in py-sdk-core under py/tests/test_pattern_*.py (notably test_pattern_feishu_thread_session.py and test_pattern_feishu_app_session.py) which import these .py files as modules and assert on the compiled topology shape. Scenarios are executed by py/tests/test_cli_scenario_exec.py against a fake runtime.
